summary: [GitHub Migration] - Decommission EC2 nodes in all OrderManager environments
description_text: Objective Decommission the EC2 nodes previously used for CI/CD in all OrderManager environments following the GitHub migration. Description With the migration to GitHub Actions complete, the legacy EC2 build/runner nodes are no longer required. This ticket covers the safe decommissioning of these resources across all OrderManager environments (dev, staging, production) to reduce infrastructure costs and maintenance overhead. Acceptance Criteria [ ] Verify no active dependencies or jobs are still using these nodes [ ] Terminate EC2 instances in non-production environments first [ ] Terminate EC2 instances in production environment after validation [ ] Confirm cost savings in AWS billing Risks & Considerations Ensure GitHub Actions workflows are fully operational before decommissioning

summary: [GitHub Migration] - Create nightly jobs and alert failures on Teams channel
description_text: Objective Set up nightly scheduled jobs in GitHub Actions and configure failure notifications to a Microsoft Teams channel. Description As part of the GitHub migration, we need to establish nightly CI/CD jobs that run automated tests, builds, or maintenance tasks. When these jobs fail, the team should be notified immediately via Microsoft Teams to ensure quick response times. Acceptance Criteria [ ] Create GitHub Actions workflows with cron-based nightly schedules [ ] Configure appropriate nightly job triggers (e.g., full test suite, integration tests, security scans) [ ] Set up Microsoft Teams webhook integration [ ] Implement failure notification workflow that posts to Teams channel with: Job name and failure status Link to failed workflow run Brief error summary [ ] Test and validate notifications are received correctly [ ] Document the setup and configuration for future maintenance

summary: [GitHub Migration] - Investigate adding AI failure summary stage
description_text: Objective Investigate the feasibility of adding an AI-powered failure summary stage to GitHub Actions workflows. Description When CI/CD pipelines fail, developers often need to dig through lengthy logs to identify the root cause. This ticket covers research and prototyping of an AI summary stage that can: - Analyze build/test failure logs - Generate a concise, human-readable summary of what went wrong - Highlight the most likely root cause and relevant log lines - Surface actionable next steps for resolution Acceptance Criteria [ ] Research available AI tools/APIs for log analysis (GitHub Copilot, etc.) [ ] Evaluate integration options with GitHub Actions [ ] Document findings and recommendations [ ] Create a proof-of-concept if feasible

summary: Consider placing Feature flag service behind GQL subgraph
description_text: Is there benefit in using a GQL subgraph for feature flags? Some benefits may be: consistent GQL API across all services. Helps abstract the FF product selection from the UI. GQL API ideally is agnostic to FF vendor. UI codegen possible with GQL. Cons: do not want to have to change the graph on each feature flag

POC Feature Flag implementation uses a docker image for the database. This story is to change this to connect to Postgres (AWS RDS). May need to reach out to cloud teams to ensure RDS is an acceptable choice. AC: Terraform changes to provision RDS Connect FF service to AWS RDS Considerations on the current Postgres Docker image + shared volum e: Running a PostgreSQL database in a Docker container with an AWS EBS volume  can be safe for production use if specific best practices are followed , though using a managed service like Amazon RDS is generally recommended to offload operational complexity. Key Considerations for Production Safety Managed Services Alternative : For most production use cases, using Amazon RDS or Amazon Aurora (which use EBS under the hood but are fully managed by AWS) is a better choice as AWS handles maintenance, backups, and scaling. Data Persistence : You must use Docker volumes (either named volumes or bind mounts) to store the PostgreSQL data directory ( /var/lib/postgresql/data ) outside the container lifecycle. Never store data within the container's ephemeral filesystem. Orchestration : For scalable and highly available production deployments, use a container orchestration system like Kubernetes with a CSI driver (Container Storage Interface) for EBS to manage volume attachment/detachment during container restarts or moves. This helps avoid issues like "stuck" EBS volumes which require manual intervention. Performance : Use Amazon EBS-optimized EC2 instances to ensure dedicated network bandwidth for EBS volumes. Select appropriate EBS volume types, such as General Purpose SSD ( gp3 ) or Provisioned IOPS SSD ( io1 ,  io2 ,  io2  Block Express), depending on your workload's required IOPS and latency. Tune PostgreSQL's memory parameters (like  shared_buffers ) to fit within the container's memory limits to avoid Out-Of-Memory (OOM) errors. Image Versioning : Avoid using the  postgres:latest  tag. Always pin your image to a specific major a…

summary: Multi row select in grid requires a select all in the column header
description_text: User story As a user, when I am using a multi-row select grid, I want to have a "select all" option in the column header so that I can easily select all items in that column. Context The issue involves a multi-select grid feature that currently lacks a "select all" option in the column header. This functionality needs to be finalized by UX. Acceptance criteria On load of the grid all rows are unselected and the select all checkbox in the column header is unchecked When clicking the select all checkbox in the column header all rows will be selected. If a few rows were already selected they remain selected. Select all implies selecting all rows in the grid including what is not visible in the grid (IE selecting all on the server) Selecting all server side does not apply any filtering on the row data. That is up to the application using select all. (Ex select all will not automatically filter out completed inquiries) As a user scrolls and additional rows become visible in the grid, they will be selected. If the user clicks select all again, this unselects all A user can unselect a single row. When this happens the select all checkbox in the column header unselects. Other information N/A

Investigate unnecessary form re-rendering in DealerPill parent component
description_text: While fixing a visual bug in the DealerPill component, we discovered an underlying re-rendering issue in the parent form that causes layout problems. The current fix (adding  position: relative  to the DealerPill root) addresses the symptom but masks a deeper issue that should be investigated. Background The DealerPill contains a visually hidden  <input>  element that is absolutely positioned When the parent form re-renders, Chrome attempts to scroll to make the input active Without  position: relative  on the pill, the absolutely positioned input resolves to the nearest positioned ancestor (the container) Current Behavior Parent form completely re-renders (unnecessary re-render) Chrome + DockTabs attempt to scroll to the focused/active input Applied Workaround (PR merged) Added  position: relative  to the DealerPill root, which contains the absolutely-positioned input within the pill's bounds. Chrome still scrolls, but since the input is now visually within the pill, it appears to work correctly. Desired Outcome Investigate why the parent form is completely re-rendering when it shouldn't be. Optimizing this would: Improve performance by reducing unnecessary renders Fix the root cause rather than masking it with CSS Discussion

Grid Filters: Changing operator on numeric column triggers an unclearable ("background") filter
description_text: Steps to Reproduce: Visit the AC and ensure all filters are cleared and there are rows in the grid (that we can filter) Click on the Limit filter dropdown to change the default numerical operator: Select the “Equal To (=)” operator Observe that all rows are filtered out and there is no active filter present in the filter header Also observe that for the “Qty (M)” column, sometimes changing the numeric operator defaults the previously empty input to have a number  0  in it. Video: Expected Outcomes Changing the numeric operator dropdown should not trigger (add or change) a filter unless it also contains a valid Number. Changing the numeric operator dropdown should not result in a value being populated in the input field. A change to a filter must always create a “clearable” filter object

summary: Stylelint - Replace shared config ignoreFiles with .stylelintignore

summary: Stylelint autofixing for converting styles into css variables
description_text: Compatible CSS properties need to be converted to their respective design system variables. Benefit from this is that in the event of an automatic design system update to css variables, it should autofix to available properties.

summary: Add style checking automation to monorepo
description_text:
Stylelint (  ) and the CSS variables VSCode extension improve code quality by checking that CSS follows best practice. This includes enforcing the use of CSS variables in required properties. Stylelint: Install Stylint and nx-stylelint at the ui root folder Add the plugin and target default config to nx.json Define stylelint rules in a central location. Follow similar monorepo patterns as base eslint ( @mktx/eslint-config ). See below for some lint rules. Add  stylelint.config.mjs  to ui/packages/platformui/ui and ui/packages/platformui/shell-app as a starting point. The project lint file should extend the base. Check that lint rules are working. CSS Variables extension: install  “vunguyentuan.vscode-css-variables"  vscode extension add a extensions.json to .vscode folder and add this extension Add the following to .vscode/settings.json "cssVariables.languages": [
    "postcss",
    "css",
  ],
  "cssVariables.lookupFiles": [
    "**/src/**/*.module.css", "ui/packages/platformui/styles-lib/src/css/ma-theme.css"
  ],
  "stylelint.validate": [
    "css",
    "postcss",
  ], Should be able to run  nx run-many --target=stylelint  and have the 2 projects linted.

Devbox: Auth/"pnpm login" once and open multiple terminals
description_text: QoL for Devbox users Every time a user enters the  devbox shell , it does a  pnpm login . This gets very annoying when opening multiple terminals with devbox shell. Make it so that it does  pnpm login  once. Only if no logged-in user for the configured registry is detected ( pnpm whoami )

User story As a developer, when updating the ag-grid license, I want to ensure it is updated for the X-Pro 2.0 design system so that all consumers can use the latest license. As a consumer of the X-Pro 2.0 license, when I import from the design system npm package, I want to access the updated ag-grid license so that I can comply with licensing requirements. Context The issue involves updating the ag-grid license for the X-Pro 2.0 design system and other libraries that share the same license. It is important for consumers to import the license from the design system npm package. Acceptance criteria The ag-grid license must be updated in the X-Pro 2.0 design system. All other libraries using the same license should also reflect this update. Consumers of the X-Pro 2.0 license should be able to import the license from the design system npm package without issues. Other information N/A

summary: viewserver - allow filtering of table data prior to aggregations
description_text: Currently we have filtering in the viewserver that filters “what the user can see” - ie, the post-aggregation data. This is a good fit for cases like floating filters. However, this is sub-optimal for cases where the filtering could have been done pre-aggregation, reducing the dataset earlier. Add an extra stage to filter data before going into the userAgg.

summary: [Monorepo] - Add dry-run to Earthly deploy targets
description_text: Add a dry-run flag to earthly’s deply stage so we can have a full validation of the steps during a PR. This is to reduce errors that can occur when modifying Earthly files related to deployment steps. Not required for MVP

summary: [CI/CD] [Not MVP] Automate process of switching environments
description_text: We need a better way to automate switching environments, ie change to a single environment flag switches everything over

summary: [CI/CD] Transition the management of build and deploy agents to an external team
description_text: Currently UI Platform/ XPro are responsible for managing build and deploy agents (running in EC2 in AWS). However, this is not something we should be handling. Instead, the management of build nodes should be transferred to an external team that specialises in infrastructure and CI/CD pipeline management. This transition may align well with the ongoing GitHub migration, and we should assess whether it makes sense to handle this as part of that effort. Identify the appropriate external team to take over build node management. Evaluate whether the migration should be included in the GitHub migration effort. Ensure the team managing the agents installs relevant software e.g. Docker, Earthly Update our pipelines to point to new agents

summary: [CI/CD] Reduce Docker image sizes and improve build performance by optimizing the build context, reducing unnecessary files, and using lightweight base images where possible.
description_text: Currently, our Node.js builds copy a large number of UI assets into the build context, leading to wasteful storage use and slower builds. Additionally, Docker image sizes can be optimized by: Minimizing the build context to exclude unnecessary files. Using Alpine-based images or other lightweight base images where applicable. Reducing layers and optimizing caching for faster builds.

summary: [CI/CD] Replace the current PAT used for SonarQube
description_text: Currently, SonarQube is using a Personal Access Token (PAT) from a personal account. This needs to be updated to: Use a dedicated service account PAT Update the CI/CD pipeline to reference the new token securely. Work with Cloud team to have them set PAT token in Sonar

summary: [CI/CD] Define the best approach for implementing ephemeral environments to support on-demand testing of feature branches and pull requests.
description_text: To improve developer workflows and testing, we need a structured approach for ephemeral environments that automatically spin up when a PR is opened and get destroyed when the PR is closed or merged. The goal is to enable isolated testing of services and UI changes without affecting shared environments. This RFC will explore: Creating EKS namespaces dynamically based on PR names for temporary workloads. Deploying UI containers with a snapshot of the UI artifacts for the given PR. Handling state e.g. in the viewserver (PR branched MongoDB collections?).

summary: [CI/CD] Evaluate GitOps tooling, specifically ArgoCD, to determine its suitability for managing our deployments and infrastructure as code.
description_text: Assess GitOps workflows and whether tools like ArgoCD align with our deployment and infrastructure management needs. This involves: Understanding how ArgoCD or other GitOps tools integrate with our current CI/CD pipeline. Evaluating the benefits of a declarative, Git-driven deployment model.Identifying potential challenges or limitations specific to our environment. Determining if GitOps would improve reliability, auditability, and automation for our deployments. Findings can be documented in an RFC Cloud team use ArgoCD so it is worth discussing with them

summary: [CI/CD] Implement a more secure and scalable approach for managing environment variables outside of CI, ensuring better integration with Terraform, EKS services, and UI applications.
description_text: Currently, environment variables are tightly coupled with CI/CD, which is not ideal for maintainability. Variables are added in ADO libraries and then passed into the CI/CD process in Earthly. This is fine for a small number of variables but since we rely on a lot of variables in Terraform, this is messy. This ticket aims to improve the management of environment variables by: Allowing Terraform to use a provider that fetches environment variables directly from Azure DevOps (ADO) or GitHub, reducing reliance on hardcoded values. Implementing an environment service for EKS services and UI applications, enabling dynamic configuration management outside of CI. ( I believe this service was started already. We can perhaps have a separate ticket to review and complete this piece ) Tasks: Investigate Terraform providers that support fetching environment variables from ADO/GitHub. Implement the Terraform provider for managing infrastructure configurations. Ensure secure storage and retrieval of environment variables. Update deployment pipelines to integrate with the new solution. Document the changes and provide guidelines for usage.

summary: [CI/CD] Migrate CI/CD to Cloud-Managed AWS Roles
description_text: Currently, build and deploy agents are running as root, which poses a security risk. Additionally, the sudo configuration is overly permissive, allowing excessive privileges that leads to security vulnerabilities. To mitigate these risks, we need to: Create dedicated non-root users for build and deployment processes. Restrict sudo permissions to only necessary commands. Ensure all necessary permissions for build and deployment processes are granted without relying on full root access. Validate that these changes do not break existing CI/CD workflows. However, if the GitHub migration or external team takeover happens soon, this change may not be worth implementing.

summary: [CI/CD] Harden security for build and deploy agent users by eliminating root execution and restricting sudo permissions.
description_text: Currently, build and deploy agents are running as root, which poses a security risk. Additionally, the sudo configuration is overly permissive, allowing excessive privileges that leads to security vulnerabilities. To mitigate these risks, we need to: Create dedicated non-root users for build and deployment processes. Restrict sudo permissions to only necessary commands. Ensure all necessary permissions for build and deployment processes are granted without relying on full root access. Validate that these changes do not break existing CI/CD workflows. However, if the GitHub migration or external team takeover happens soon, this change may not be worth implementing.

summary: [CI/CD] Set up nightly Azure DevOps pipeline jobs to ensure all services are built and deployed to the dev environment.
description_text: We need to configure nightly execution of our Azure DevOps pipeline jobs to automate the build and deployment of all services to the dev (QA too?) environment. The likely approach is to add a scheduled trigger to the existing pipeline jobs while ensuring: All relevant services are included in the nightly build and deployment process. The scheduling is properly configured to run at an appropriate time (e.g., outside working hours).
